entry.title: Community project: Selection of classic UI tools
entry.content:
Collection of tools for the classic dynpro UI of SAP

The provided class ZCL_UITOOLS can be used via inheritance or as an instance.

Manipulate screen fields in PBO

Instead of using loop at screen., you can now use the following calls to influence on the screen behaviour. The following examples are based on a class that inheritates from ZCL_UITOOLS

" hide a field by group or name
group( 'GR1' )->hide( ).
field( 'MATNR' )->hide( ).
" hide a selection or a parameter of a report screen
selection( 'S_MATNR' )->hide( ).
" set fields to no-input
group( 'GR1' )->set_noinput( ).
field( 'MATNR' )->set_noinput( ).
" make a field obligatory
group( 'GR1' )->set_required( ).
field( 'MATNR' )->set_required( ).
 

Set dropdown values in PBO

Using the field( ) method, you can set dropdown values for a list box

  field( 'GROUPNR' )->set_dropdown_values(
    value #(
      ( key = '1' text = 'First group' )
      ( key = '2' text = 'Second group' )
      ( key = '3' text = 'Third group' )
      ( key = '4' text = 'Forth group' ) ) ).
 

Simple Yes/No (/Cancel) queries

The methods ask( ) and ask_with_cancel( ) provide a simple interface to POPUP_TO_CONFIRM

if ask( question = 'Is this OK?' ).
  message 'OK' type 'I'.
else.
  message 'not OK' type 'I'.
endif.
" version that displays also a cancel button
try.
    if ask_with_cancel( question = 'Is this OK?' ).
      message 'OK' type 'I'.
    else.
      message 'not OK' type 'I'.
    endif.
  catch zcx_uitools_user_cancelled into data(e).
    message e type 'I' display like 'E'.
endtry.
 

Both methods provide also an optional parameter LONGTEXT where you can pass a longtext object created with SE61

Demo report

Explore ZP_UITOOLS_DEMO to learn more

dependencies

The coding uses https://github.com/abapify/throw and https://github.com/abapify/assert

entry.title: Scrolling table control
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a screen that contains a table, which should be populated by default when the screen is opened. So, I put my selection logic in the PBO event, and it retrieves and displays the data when the screen opens.

But the problem is that when I scroll up or down in the table, it navigates back to the previous screen.

Regards,

entry.title: S/4 Public cloud - Service - solution order and repair in-house
entry.content:
Dear experts,

My customer has the following requirement : we are implementing S/4HANA Public cloud Service module. My customer maintains customer's equipments but for some equipments, the business process steps are : 

1. A technician goes to customer's site in order to dismantle the equipment on customer production line and the technician bring the equipment in-house in our repair center or the customer send it via a transport company to our repair center.

2. The dismantle equipment will be repaired in house and checked. And the equipment will be sent to the customer.

3. The dismantle equipment is installed on the customer production line by a technician and checked.

From a customer point of view, it's a global repair  with only one invoice, I need to manage 2 service order (for step 1 and step 3) and 1 repair. I need to manage the planning and the execution of each steps, following the cost...

I though I was able to manage it with a solution order in order to manage the 3 steps. I didn't find any document regarding solution order and repair in-house. How can I generate an in-house repair process from a solution order ?

For a workaround, I wanted to link repairs and service order in the transaction history tab of the service order. But it's not possible.

How do you manage this process in S/4HANA public cloud ? or what is the workaround in order to answer the customer's requirement.

Thanks a lot

entry.title: OEE Calculation – Performance & Quality Loss Not Captured
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need your support in completing my business scenario for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation in SAP DMC.

I have configured all the required master data and settings, and availability is being calculated correctly for my resources. However, I am facing issues where Performance Loss (Untagged Losses) and Quality Loss are not being captured in the OEE calculation.

🔹 Performance Loss Issue:

  • I have maintained the standard rates for resources and configured the Shift Schedule (Day Shift with Scheduled Breaks), and assigned to Resource. 
  • The operation was started and completed with an additional 1-hour extra time to account for untagged losses (assumed as Performance Loss), yet no values are updated in Time Loss.

🔹 Quality Loss Issue:

  • I have booked quantity confirmations and recorded scrap/rejected quantities with reason codes, but no impact is seen on OEE.
  • I also recorded a Non-Conformance (NC) and later scrapped the SFC, but in this case, the entire SFC was scrapped, preventing further processing.

Could you please suggest if any settings or steps might be missing in my configuration to ensure Performance Loss and Quality Loss are properly reflected in OEE?

Looking forward to your guidance.

@ManoelCosta @sunita_sarkar

entry.title: Re: Payroll PCR to get Weekly/Annual Working hours
entry.content: the valuation basis is calculated correctly. The issue is IT0007 Annual working hours is hard coded to 2288 (52 i.e., no of weeks in a year * IT0007 no of hours worked in a week). We need to write a rule to replace the hard code value 2288. Thanks

entry.title: SAP CIS Identity Provisioning Service integration with SAP HANA Cloud, HANA Database
entry.content:
This blog post is dedicated to the functionality available from Q32024 and which was long awaited by a lot of customers.

The Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) integration, a part of SAP Cloud Identity Services, is now available for SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database (HANA DB) (link to a public HANA Cloud roadmap item and a link IPS “What is new” section).

This integration allows for streamlined automated user provisioning, simplifying the management of user access and roles within the HANA Cloud environment. IPS, as a mechanism for assignments transport, maps assignments coming from user source, such as the Identity Directory, to the one in HANA Cloud DB (like HANA roles). By leveraging this functionality, organizations can enhance their identity management processes directly within their cloud database systems.

In this blog post, we will dwell through detailed guidance on configuring and utilizing this IPS service together with HANA DB.

Prerequisites

IPS features a connector for the HANA DB. This plug-in allows for the management of users and their role assignments. When using the SAP HANA Database as the target system, the Identity Provisioning service will manage all users in the user group(s) where the technical user of the service has the OPERATOR privilege. It is possble to set up the same SAP HANA database as a target multiple times in order to manage different user groups, by defining different technical users for the IPS.

Set up in SAP BTP Cockpit to access IPS as a part of SAP CIS

First of all, you need an access to SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS) tenant, as IPS is a part of it. Above all, please check if you have already assigned CIS tenant, you can do it by following the steps explained here.

If not available, you would be able to get your productive and test tenant as part of a self-service request in SAP BTP cockpit by following the next procedure described in SAP CIS initial set up guide.

Second of all, you would need to configure SAP HANA Cloud “admin-api-access“ service plan (XSUAA instance) to gain access to the Identity Provisioning Service. For that you must have:

  1. SAP BTP global account with a , including the admin-api-access service plan. If you still do not have an account, please see Getting a Global Account and View and Manage Services from the Service Marketplace.
  2. You must have the Subaccount Administrator role collection for the subaccount. Speak to your SAP BTP global account administrator if you do not have this one.
  3. After that, you need to subscribe to the admin-api-access service plan and create a service binding for it. How to do it, we will review in the procedure below.

Procedure

  1. In SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to the subaccount where you want to create the subscription to the SAP HANA Cloud multi-environment administration tools.
  2. Create a subscription to the SAP HANA Cloud administration API service plan:
    1. In the left pane of the subaccount, click “Instances and Subscription” and Click “Create”.
    2. Under Service, pick “SAP HANA Cloud”.
    3. Under Plan, pick admin-api-access.
    4. Under “Runtime environment”, pick “other” for multi-environment option. 



    5. Click “Next” to continue and enter the configuration parameters as JSON:

 

 

 

{ 
"technicalUser": true, 
}

 

 

 

"technicalUser": This is a mandatory parameter which configures whether this client is a technical user. A technical user is a special user for whom all scopes are set as authorities. If the parameter is set to true, the client grants (client credentials, client x509) return an access token that includes all scopes.

6. Click “Create” to finish the creation of the instance.

3. Create a service binding:

1. In your subaccount, navigate to the “Services” -> “Instances and Subscriptions” page.

2. Go to the “Instances” tab and locate your instance. Click the *** (Actions Menu) icon of the admin-api-access service instance you created and select “Create Service Binding” (Multi-environment) or “Create Service Key” (Cloud Foundry) depending on the subscription chosen above.

3. In the “New Binding” wizard, choose a name for your binding and provide configuration parameters either by uploading a JSON file or by entering them in the dialog. For our demo scenario, let’s use the next configuration:

 

 

 

{
  "credential-type": "binding-secret",
  "hide-secret": true
}

 

 

 

For more information on service binding parameters, see Binding Parameters of SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service.

4. You have subscribed to the admin-api-access service plan and created a new binding for the service instance.

1. Retrieve “the client ID”, “client secret” and “url” through the service binding on the “Instances and Subscriptions “page of your subaccount by clicking on the “View Credentials” button.

2. You will need these values to configure the OAuth2TokenServiceURL, 'User' and 'Password' properties when configuring the 'SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database' system in SAP Identity Provisioning Service. You will also need some of this information to configure your technical user in the SAP HANA database.

Set up in SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database (HANADB)

  1. For setup of the HANADB, client-id, certificate and issuer are important that can be obtained using the url retrieved in the above step 4.a. To get the certificate and issuer, you need to visit the  location: {url}/sap/trust/jwt.

Sample response of location: {url}/sap/trust/jwt:

 

 

 

{
  "issuer_dn": "OU=JWT, CN=https://<random>.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token",
  "subject_dn": "OU=JWT, CN=https://<random>.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token",
  "purpose": "JWT",
  "certificate": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----<encoded-certificate>==-----END CERTIFICATE-----",
  "issuer": "https://<random>.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token"
}

 

 

 

2. To successfully configure JWT authentication trust to your XSUAA client must be established. This process requires the XSUAA certificate to be available. For that, we can use our guided JWT provider creation wizard in Cloud Central. You only need to provide the issuer URL of your XSUAA. The wizard will also make suggestions for all names, so you only need to provide a name for the provider. The detailed guide how to use it you can find in the following 2-parts series blog-post.

Alternatively, the next sql commands can be used from below. They contain some placeholders in angle brackets. These placeholders have to be replaced as follows:

  • identity, issuer, certificate:
    • The values are extracted from the previous step. Please note “identity” value is client-id, “issuer” is the same as issuer and “certificate” is the same as certificate.
  • certificateName, pseName, userName, usergroupName, jwtProviderName:
    • The values are only used internally and can be chosen accordingly
    • Suggested values are (replacing PRODUCT by something that identifies your product) 
      • certificateName SCIM_<PRODUCT>_CERTIFICATE 
      • pseName SCIM_<PRODUCT>_PSE 
      • userName SCIM_<PRODUCT>_USER 
      • usergroupName SCIM_<PRODUCT>_GROUP 
      • jwtProviderName SCIM_<PRODUCT>_PROVIDER 

3. Open HC Central SQL console and connect to the HANA database any user having the CREATE USERGROUP (for user group creation) and TRUST ADMIN (for certificate maintenance) system privileges. 

Issue the following sql commands to create (if not done yet with Guided JWT provider creation wizard): 

  • PSE (certificate collection) along with the trusted certificates 
  • JWT provider for your authentication that uses the PSE to verify the validity of JWTs: 

 

 

 

create certificate <certificateName> from '<certificate>' 
create pse <pseName> 
alter pse <pseName> add certificate <certificateName> 
create jwt provider with issuer '' claim 'client_id' as external identity claim 'client_id'='' priority 210 
set pse <pseName> purpose jwt for provider <jwtProviderName>

 

 

 

4. At this step, we are going to create a new usergroup for IPS -managed users and technical database user IPS/SCIM to manage the users. We also configure JWT authentication for the user in which we map the client-id value from the admin-api-access to the database user. 

In the example below the user is part of the user group DEFAULT but you can choose to add it to any other user group. The technical user is set as an OPERATOR for the new SCIM user group. This can be done through GUI but for completeness, here are the SQL statements that can be used. 

 

 

 

create usergroup <usergroupName> 
create user <userName> with identity '<identity>' for jwt provider <jwtProviderName> set usergroup default 
grant usergroup operator on usergroup <usergroupName> to <userName>

 

 

 

5. Finally, the new user created in step-4 is assigned the ROLE ADMIN system privilege to be able to grant/revoke roles. You don‘t need to use ROLE ADMIN but can also use role groups if the IPS/SCIM user should only manage a subset of roles. 

 

 

 

grant create schema to <userName> 
grant role admin to <userName> 
GRANT CATALOG READ TO <userName>

 

 

 

IPS set up for SAP HANA Cloud, HANA DB

  1. First and foremost, please get yourself familiar with IAM reference architecture as a starting point. As per our reference architecture, the strategy is to have the Identity Directory (also a component of SAP CIS) as a user source (even if you use other solutions as Microsoft Entra ID). 
  2. Then, please go to Identity provisioning app to set up HANA Cloud as your “Source” or/and “Target” systems as per your use case. If you want to do identity provisioning from one HANA (source) to another HANA (target), configuration on both HANAs is needed, as with the following parameters.  

Property Name 

Description & Value 

Authentication 

Enter: BasicAuthentication 

hana.cloud.db.instance.type 

Enter: hdb 

hana.cloud.db.instance.id 

Refers to a unique identifier associated with a specific database instance within the SAP HANA Cloud. 

 

OAuth2TokenServiceURL 

Enter the OAuth 2.0 Token Service URL. 

For example: https://demo-hc-3-test.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token 

Password 

(Credential) Enter the OAuth Client Secret, created for your SAP HANA Database system. 

ProxyType 

Enter: Internet 

Type 

Enter: HTTP 

URL 

Enter the SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database API URL. 

For example: https://api.gateway.orchestration.<cluster>-<datacenter>.hanacloud.ondemand.com 

User 

Enter the OAuth Client Id, created for your SAP HANA Database system. 

Note: Please use the following values for the three parameters as mentioned in the service-binding procedure shown above in step 4.a. (when creating your admin-api-access): 

  1. “OAuth2TokenServiceUrl” as the URL  
  2. “User” as the Clientid 
  3. “Password” as the Clientsecret 

There are few additional optional parameters as shown in the screenshot below that help with debugging. Please refer IPS Documentation for more details on the configuration parameters and transformations. 

3. Trigger the Jobs as per your need: 

Read Job:  

Trigger the read job to provision only the new and updated users from the source to the target. After the read job, new users will be created in target and the state of certain users will be updated depending on the differences. For further information on the read job please refer to the document.  

Resync job:  

This job is specifically used to fix inconsistencies between two systems. It reads full entities from source and updates the entities in target to be in sync with source.  At the end of the sync job both the source and target will have same state in respective to resources - users and Groups(roles) in HANA. Refer the document for further details on sync job. 

The two jobs above are mainly used to manage users. For other jobs please refer to the CIS/IPS documentation: 

  1. Simulate Job (estimates the number of entities that will be created, updated, deleted or skipped)  
  2. Validate Job (verifies the content of the entities) 

4. The output of step-2 would look like following with successful Jobs done: 

With that, you will conclude the provisioning of your users to desired systems.

---

Additional helpful sources:

IAS side: 

What's New for Cloud Identity Services 

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database (Source) 

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database (Target) 

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database (Proxy) 

For Neo environment: https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database 

the HANA DB beta connector is the one for the HANA DB on prem version.  Among other limitations, this connector is only available for standalone SCI tenants running on the NEO infrastructure. More details you can read on the help page. 

HANA Cloud side: 

Admin guides: 

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/user-provisioning-with-identity-provisioning-service (Setup in SAP BTP Cockpit) 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-administration-guide/user-provisioning (Setup In SAP HANA database)   

Security Guide: 

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-security-guide/user-administration-tools 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-security-guide/sap-identity-provisioning-service

entry.title: Availability in S/4HANA public cloud about KBA 3351929 - Business Partner Extensions for Japan VAT
entry.content:
Hi

Does anyone know if the following Japanese specific field in the supplier master can be added in S/4HANA Public cloud as same as S/4HANA on-premise?

"Japan: Valid Period for Tax Registration available in the tab Vendor: Country Specific Enh. 3.

We use SAP MDG(Master Data Governance) system and replicate business partner master between S/4HANA on-premise and S/4HANA public cloud.
And now there is a request to have the country specific field "Japan: Valid Period for Tax Registration available in the tab Vendor: Country Specific Enh. 3. " in S/4 public could which is available in S/4 on-premise by adopting the following KBA.

3351929 - Business Partner Extensions for Japan VAT

If it is possible, I would appreciate how to make it available in S/4HANA public cloud.

Thank you!

entry.title: Create a single credit note to a dummy customer based on different customers sales and per SKU
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to ask, is it possible and how to do in SAP the following scenario:

- create one credit note with a manually entered price to a dummy client (e.g. a national agency)

- there are no sales to this dummy client

- the credit note servers the purpose of a discount provided to this client, a rebate agreement is not used

- the amount of the credit note is different every time

- the credit note should be based on the past quarter sales to different customers and based on a single SKU (which is sold to different customers)

- the manually entered amount is distributed over the sales to these customers per SKU proportionally by the sales amount achieved by each customer 

- at the end, when running a sales report - it should show the sales per customer and per SKU after the discount to match FI side

- the G/L used should be for revenue with a discount, so this credit note reduces the sales revenue at the end

- the aim is to go through SD with a sales document type, as from FI side there is no report which shows sales after discount per SKU, unless there is a solution how to do that only from FI side

Is there any way to do that, or through S/4HANA analytics or another...

Thank you!

entry.title: S/4 Public cloud - Service - recurring service for in-house repair
entry.content:
Dear experts,

My customer has the following requirement and we are implementing S/4HANA public cloud Service module. We have seen the 4X5 scope item. Because the main activity of my customer is to repair in house the customer's equipment, with the maintenance plans, I need to generate an object in order to communicate to the customer that he should send me his equipment, and then process an in-house repair process.

I only have the possibility to generate service order so my question is : how can I generate an object which can trigger an in-house repair process ? If I encode a repair template, the system whant to generate a SVO1 service order. But I don't want this because it's not a repair process.

How can we manage this process in S/4 public cloud ? or what is the workaround ?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: Re: Rebate Accruals validity period issue
entry.content:
We have had the same Problem. I found OSS note 

3445915 - How-to control the business volume using valid from and valid to field to apply a rebate rate

with the setting in this note describe. I set the value to 1.

You can maintain the valid from and to Dates for the business volume selection

Example 

31.01.2023 Delta Accruals 01.01.2023 31.01.2023
28.02.2023 Delta Accruals 01.02.2023 28.02.2023
31.03.2023 Delta Accruals 01.03.2023 31.03.2023
30.04.2023 Delta Accruals 01.04.2023 30.04.2023
31.05.2023 Delta Accruals 01.05.2023 31.05.2023
30.06.2023 Partial Settlement

regards

Sabine

entry.title: IAS + ONB (Welcome Email Day 1)
entry.content:
Hi folks!

We have in place the onboardees login managed by IAS including the Provisioning Real Time Sync. So at the moment that the Onboarding Process starts the onboardee is created at IAS Platform. IAS is sending the Welcome Emails and they are able to login to access, so all good.

Question is:

Is there any way to send an additional welcome email once the onboardee becomes into an employee?

Once the onboardee is created into IAS, it has a IAS User Profile and it receives a Welcome Email thanks to:

 

{
  "targetPath": "$.sendMail",
  "condition": "$.userType == 'ONBOARDEE'",
  "constant": "943fec28-xxxx-xxxx-8799-aaee814453c6",
  "scope": "createEntity"
},

 

So during the "CreateEntity" the email is sent.

The thing is that during the Hiring Process, HR changes the onboardee data like the "username" and the "email" and those actions are replicated to the IAS User Profile (expected behaviour)

Our concerns is that at the moment the onboardee becomes into a employee, there is not another "CreateEntity" event (we can add the transformation, but doesn't work) it's only an UPDATE into the IAS User Profile, so the user (now is an employee) doesn't know the new username or email due to it was changed during the Hiring Process and is not the original one that he/she received as onboardee.

Is there any way to detect when someone change at IAS side from onboardee to employee in order to send another Welcome Emails with the current username value?

Regards,

Jose Alberto Vargas

entry.title: Re: Not able to change the flexible Workflow once it is a...
entry.content:
Hi,

The binding cannot be edited in the SWDD_SCENARIO screen once the runtime version is activated.

For changing the binding, please follow the following steps - 

1. Go to PFTC transaction. Select Workflow Template and enter the workflow number without 'WS' and click on edit.

2. Go to 'Triggering Events' tab and click on Binding Editor Button (pls refer the attached image).

3. We can change the binding there and save. 

Thank you.

entry.title: daily scheduled job for V_V2 assigns goods to sales orders
entry.content: The daily scheduled job for V_V2 sometimes assigns goods to sales orders that are actually not available (neither in stock nor with scheduled deliveries). If the availability check is manually performed on the individual impacted orders the next day, the assignment occurs correctly, but it is altered again with the nightly execution of the job. From the reports received, we have noticed that the codes involved are often linked to sales orders and intercompany OdTs at the same time. Furthermore, we have been told that the problem seems to resolve itself spontaneously after invoicing one of the sales orders involved. To temporarily manage the situation, we used the SDV03V01 and SDRQCR21 reports, which in most cases led to a realignment of the assignments.

thank you for reaching out in the SAP Community. 

You can use the Manage Operating Charts of Accounts app for your use case. With the Expert Worklist and the proper authorization, you can download a spreadsheet template to enter the information for multiple new G/L accounts to be created. This spreadsheet can be uploaded later with all necessary information for newly to be created G/L accounts.

More information can be found in the SAP Help Portal.

Best regards,

Anja

Not just to avoid screenshots. It works more like this – the more readable the object is, the less screenshots are needed. However, the list of objects is primarily needed for the agent to be able to interact with interface elements. For example, to press a button, its identifier must be listed.

entry.title: Re: User Data considerations when implementing Employee Central with Learning

@ChelseyMarzolf thank you for raising this point, this is indeed a limitation that was reported to the team responsible for the instance refresh tool. Best solutions are being evaluated for future releases.
Meanwhile you need to go through the manual refresh process for Learning.

If anyone advises you to set-up the Employee Export job while you already have an export job in Integration Center - PLEASE DO NOT PROCEED. This recommendation is incorrect and could lead to unwanted data in your system because both jobs process certain fields differently.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Enable HTTPS port on SAP JAVA - SSL port not active
entry.content:
Hi,

My PO system can now listen on the HTTPS port.

Issue was most likely the parameters I added from SAP note 1951806, but I had to restart the entire server. After server restart, and PO start, the HTTPS port showed green.

This worked without any SSL certs installed.

After HTTPS service shown as green, I then was able to upload a wildcard domain cert which was provided by the client.  PO URL now shows as secure.

Thanks.

entry.title: Are you looking for learning martial for SAP IBP platform for time series-based heuristics planning?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Mastering the main features in infinite time-series Network Planning  learning journey.

Overview
This course is designed to immerse you in the SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) platform's essential features and master time series-based heuristics planning.
You will explore the core functionalities and capabilities of the SAP IBP platform. Learn how to navigate the system, configure key components, and leverage the integration and analytics tools to manage your planning processes more effectively.
Engage in hands-on exercises that give you practical experience configuring and using these powerful planning tools. You'll gain the skills needed to implement and manage effective planning strategies, driving improved accuracy and efficiency in your supply chain operations. 

Learning objectives
By the end of this course, you'll be proficient in using the SAP IBP platform for time series-based heuristics planning, equipped to boost your organization's planning processes and confidently make data-driven decisions. You'll discover how to set up and utilize time series data for accurate forecasting and planning in time series-based heuristics planning. You'll also understand how to configure planning operators and heuristics to optimize planning scenarios, ensuring efficient and reliable demand and supply balancing.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring Planning Processes in SAP IBP
  • Exploring the Tools & Features in SAP IBP
  • Discovering SAP IBP for S&OP

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Point Of View - SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
• Introduction 

SAP has introduced Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 as part of its Product Portfolio Evolution in SAP CX. This new version offers modernization approach, more advance business capabilities compared to existing SAP Sales and Service Core V1 (also known SAP C4C).

This point of view write-up is to address basic key points regarding product understanding, consulting, business approach and solution design including Why is there a need for V2? What is it? What value does it add ? Adoption approach/consideration for V2. 

  • The Context – Why SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2

Given the challenges posed by macroeconomic disruptions, business model transitions and technology transformations it’s crucial to address these needs in Sales and Service LoB. By providing more intelligent insight to connect with customers, capture emerging trends, data driven guidance, scale operations faster and delivering greater profitability.

  • There is need to deliver modern technology platform, which is more agile, robust, scalable etc.
  • The current platform V1 is overstretched from cloud design standards.
  • Common issues about existing platform are about Performance, inflexibility, landscape extensibility, UX design, Intelligent User experience.
  •  What is SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2
  • Architecture and Benefits – Cloud Native Stack (CNS)

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 is built on modernized platform to meet high-value demands of SAP Customer Experience (CX) journeys. It supports comprehensive processes such as “Problem to Resolution”, “Campaign to Cash” and “Commerce to Delivery” which ensures seamless customer interactions throughout the entire lifecycle.

 Solution Feature Highlights:

  • Flexible platform which provides agility and empower sales and service operations.
  • Built on modern Cloud Native Stack (CNS) leveraging SAP BTP platform.
  • Microservices Based Architecture
  • SAP Business AI – to leverage Gen AI, ML
  • Host on Hyperscaler
  • Side-by-Side extensions, Low-Code/No-Code UI tools
  • Improved Cloud Qualities and Advanced Business Capabilities
  • Increased Performance, shorter time-to-market

       The architecture of V2 is designed with a microservice, API and Event based approach, this modern approach leverages benefits of hyperscalers ensuring scalability, flexibility. Additionally, it complements the core solution with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), enabling creation of  composable apps according to business needs.

Reference: SAP( https://saplearninghub.plateau.com/learning -Webinar: SAP Sales Cloud V2   with SAP Business AI – Consultant bootcamp)

Benefits: V2 offers numerous business benefits to scale operations faster, innovative user experience, provides embedded intelligence in making informed decisions and improved productivity, high performance, pre-built connectors to reduce implementation time and effort  

                                                           

Reference: https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/SAP+Service+Cloud+V2+%E2%80%93+A+New+Paradigm+-+Webcasts/1_fvqsv4g2          

  • SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 feature overview: 
  • Guided Selling: Brings structured selling process powered by embedded insights
  • Pipeline Management and Forecasting: View of pipeline health, more accurate forecast tracking & monitoring
  • Customer Insight: Capture information of business partners, Timeline view, Tailor and Configure workspace according to need
  • Productivity: Timeline based 360-degree view of customer process, Activity Management
  • Intelligent Mobile Sales: Native mobile apps for sales engagements.
  • Modern User Experience: better structured view, quick access.

Refer SAP Roadmap explorer (https://roadmaps.sap.com/) to get more insight on delivered features and upcoming roadmap plan for feature availability.

Reference: SAP ( https://saplearninghub.plateau.com/learning -Webinar: SAP Sales Cloud V2

  • SAP Service Cloud Version 2 feature overview:
  • Agent Desktop / Customer Hub: Improves efficiency, one click access to connected processes
  • Case Management: Agile and guided step process along with Case Designer to configure case
  • Advance Productivity and Performance: Email Management, Automate repetitive task with service intelligence and automated flows
  • Global Search: Elastic search capabilities to execute complex queries extremely fast
  • Service Intelligence: Embedded AI for case categorization, case summarization, machine translations
  • Embedded Analytics: SAP Analytics cloud inclusion for instant operational visibility
  • Service Cloud for Utilities add-on: Modern processes tailored for utilities 

Refer SAP Roadmap explorer (https://roadmaps.sap.com/) to get more insight on delivered features and upcoming roadmap plan for feature availability. 

Reference: SAP ( https://saplearninghub.plateau.com/learning -Webinar: SAP Service Cloud V2 )

  •    AI based Business Intelligence:

Sales and Service Cloud V2 solution includes various SAP Business AI embedded capabilities to provide better insight, automation and productivity optimization:

  • Prediction (AI/ML): Analyze business data for score key metric, provide recommendations.
  • Generative (Gen AI): In context of business data and business process flow
  • Conversational (Chatbot): AI driven chatbot to answer request by using natural language processing.

There are various built-in features provided for e.g. in Sales Cloud (Lead and Opportunity scoring, Relationship Intelligence proposal for Contact, Product Recommendation, Visit insights), similarly in Service Cloud (Case Categorization, Email sentiment detection, Case summarization, Email recommendation).

 Reference: SAP( https://saplearninghub.plateau.com/learning - Webinar: SAP Sales Cloud V2)

  •   Integration Technology:

For an end-to-end business process integration strategy, SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 can be seamlessly incorporated into broader SAP ecosystem using “Out-of-the-box” integration with predefined integration content. These integrations include :                                                                                              

  1. SAP BTP                                                                                                                                       
  2. SAP Cloud Integration (CI)                                                                                                            
  3. SAP Event Mesh, API                                                                                                             
  4. UI & Data Mashups                               

 Pre-packaged integration with other products in SAP CX suite:

  • Integration with S/4 HANA Cloud / On-Premise
  • Integration with SAP ECC6 (EHP8)
  • Integration with CPQ
  • Integration with SAP Emarsys                                                                                                               
  • Custom Developments:

To extend standard applications or build custom ones, SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 offers Low-Code/No-Code development tools. This tool includes:

  • Key User Tools:  Create extension fields, UI Adaptations, Custom Validation, and     Determinations
  •  SAP Build: Side-by-Side Extensibility using Low Code/No Code App, Mashup, Prototyping, Multichannel support
  • SAP BTP: Leverage features of BTP platform to decouple logic outside core for eg. CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model), RAP(Restfull Application Programming), UI5 etc
  • Microservices: Provided by SAP, Microsoft etc
  •  Possible adoption approach for V2:

With the introduction of this product, several questions arise regarding how to decide and plan approach on V2 adaptation. Enterprises need to analyze various scenarios based on their current state of CRM usage. 

The first question to ask and understand is - Where are we?

  • Currently using SAP On-premise or legacy CRM and planning to transition to SAP CX solutions
  • Already on stable V1 environment with all rollouts completed for markets/region
  • On V1 with pilot countries and plan for to onboard more market/countries
  • On V1 with ECC as backend, where S/4 transition is planned.
  • Integration setup of V1 with SAP /Non-SAP products

           Way Forward…            

  • Understand existing scope with reference to below aspect, along with future business roadmap.

  • Classify your existing functionalities into “must-have” and “nice-to-have” categories and then compare them with capabilities of V2 to determine fitment.
  • As-is Customizations and it’s transition impact while moving from V1 to V2
  • Data Migration support for transition of master and business documents
  • Existing integration methodology and impact to it considering V2 framework.   

          Assess adaptability options…

Once there is clarity on various aspects mentioned above, it’s crucial to assess the adoption plan of V2. Here are couple of considerations: 

  • V2 Migration / Implementation Options:  Complete/Partial/ Re-implementation
  • Dual approach, to run V1 and V2 in parallel
  • When planning tenant strategy for V2, consider factor such as geography, business units. SAP provides options to purchase SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 either in same tenant or different tenant depend on needs
  • Change Management plan with key stakeholders
  • Evaluate various decision parameters including onboarding speed, functionality support, stability, performance, migration path, dependency with back-office systems, security, and cost.
  •  Planning Transition from core V1 to V2:

While planning transition journey from V1 to Sales and Service Cloud V2:

  • SAP has introduced various assistant tools and professional service offerings to support the transition to V2.       
  • Readiness Check Tool: This summarizes solution implementation details in existing V1 and provides feature parity, detail info about transition support from V1 to V2, complexity
  • Data Transfer Tool: The Data Transfer tool supports in transitioning data from SAP Cloud for Customer to SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and SAP Service Cloud Version 2. It provides a transition tool with connection between SAP Cloud for Customer (V1) and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 (V2) for automatic data transfer.
    • The following scenarios are supported by the Data Transfer tool:
    • Single Go-Live implementation
    • Core data transition
    • Extension fields, Notes, and Attachments are supported for transition when mapping is completed by business objects. For more information, see the respective business object transition documentation for detailed scope.

Below image outlines transition phases and associated activities for successful transition   journey from V1 to V2.                        

  • Closing Comments

The SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 solution, built on a modern technology platform, making it more powerful to meet current demands and future advancements in sales and customer services. It provides standardized business processes, extensive customization options, business AI, and seamless integrations with other products. 

As part of the transformation process to V2, SAP is introducing additional tools to assist in transitioning from Core V1 to V2. Customers can benefit from a comprehensive readiness analysis check and can embark on this journey with the support of internal teams, implementation partners, and SAP. This approach ensures feature parity, effective change management, and informed decision-making, ultimately enhancing customer satisfaction and operational efficiency.

entry.title: SAP Universal ID Deletion and Unlinking Uncovered
entry.content:
What to know more about requesting UID deletion or unlinking an email or user ID from your SAP Universal ID, what it means and what are the implications. If so, please read on …. 

 

 

First of all, quick recap  

What is an SAP Universal ID? 

The SAP Universal ID is a wallet to manage your SAP user identities. It ensures lifelong access to your account and preserves your reputation such as learning certificates and community content. 

 

SAP Universal ID is your personal SAP identity while the S-user ID is a Business SAP identity given by company administrators, and can be utilized as an entitlement under the SAP Universal ID. 

 

How to create a UID and link users?  

Visit the SAP Universal ID registration page and follow the instructions. 

During registration, you will be forwarded to the Linking Manager which will retrieve the S/P-user accounts that match the e-mail that you have verified. 

If you already have an SAP Universal ID, you can link additional S/P-user ID(s) accounts anytime in SAP Universal ID Account Manager. 

 

Detailed instructions can be found in this KBA 2882908 - How to add an additional user or e-mail address to the SAP Universal ID (UID) and in this video here. 

 

Deleting and Unlinking explained 

At times you may wish to delete your UID, unlink users or remove the email accounts linked to your UID. So how do you go about doing this ……

1. How to unlink an S-user or P-user from an SAP Universal ID.  

Contact CIC by clicking here and provide the following: 

  • Primary UID email address: 
  • User to unlink (S-user ID, P-user ID): 
  • Reason for the request: 

Important: the e-mail must be sent from the same e-mail address linked to the Universal ID and/or match the same e-mail address on an account (S-user ID, P-user ID) that you are requesting to be unlinked. 

What will it mean if I unlink a user from my UID?  

Unlinking of users will have no impact on the user or your UID but remember: the email address linked to the S-user ID/ P-user ID must be removed in order to re-link associated S-user ID / P-user ID with another UID. 

2. How to remove an email address linked to an SAP Universal ID 

Contact CIC by clicking here and provide the following: 

  • Primary UID email address: 
  • Email address to be removed: 
  • Reason for the request: 

Important: the e-mail must be sent from the same e-mail address linked to the Universal ID and/or match the same e-mail address on an account (S-user ID, P-user ID) that you are requesting to be removed. 

 

What will it mean if I remove an email address from my UID?  

Removing one of your email addresses linked to your UID will remove the email and all users/accounts (P-user ID or S_user ID) associated with the email. 

Regardless of whether certifications are bound or not bound, removal of an email address will not affect the certifications linked to a specific user or Universal ID.   

Also please note that it is now a mandatory requirement to have an SAP Universal ID in place and to have a minimum of two verified e-mail addresses tied to your UID with at least one email address being linked to your recovery/personal email account. 

3. How to delete an SAP Universal ID. 

You can request account deletion within the SAP Universal ID Account Manager- Privacy page. Clicking the 'Request account deletion' button opens a new browser tab where you must provide more information in a contact form, which will then be sent to the Customer Interaction Center. 

Note: If you are unable to access the UID Account Manager directly, please contact CIC by clicking here  

And provide the following information: 

  • Primary UID email address: 
  • Delete entire UID: Yes/No 
  • Reason for the request: 

Important: the e-mail must be sent from the same e-mail address linked to the Universal ID and/or match the same e-mail address on an account (S-user ID, P-user ID) linked to your UID. 

 

What will it mean if I delete my SAP Universal ID?  

"Deleting" a Universal ID will mean it will be deleted permanently from SAP systems and has the following implications means: 

  • You won’t be able to login with your existing UID 
  • Your UID cannot be used again in the future 
  • You will not be able to manage your legacy accounts 
  • Your legacy accounts will be unlinked 
  • Your SAP Certifications will be deleted 

If Certifications are Bound to your UID: Deletion Request will not be processed - If deleted, certifications will be lost. 
If Certifications are not Bound to your UID: Deletion Request will be processed - It is safe to go ahead with request. 

 

 

For more information, please refer also to the following documentation: 

  • 2914815 - How to unlink users from an SAP Universal ID or delete the UID 
  • SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! - SAP Community 
  • 2547250 - How to reassign training certifications to a new S-user ID - SAP Training | SAP Knowledge Base Article  
  • 560499 - Customer Interaction Center: Hotline - Email – Chat 
  • Customer Interaction Center (CIC) Worldwide Telephone Directory 

Thanks for reading and I hope you found it useful! 

    

Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have.    

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community    

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors to Power BI - Connection error
entry.content:
Hi @former_member83792 ,

I had this same frustration for a long time. Power Desktop works but the moment you publish to the service you get this invalid credentials error.

The error message is misleading as you can't see the response body from Success Factors.

I found the real problem in my case was that the api user in Success Factors whitelists IP addresses and you need to get the PowerBI Service IP addresses and whitelist them:

Steps:

1. Make sure your API user is set so the password does not expire as this also results in the same opaque error.

2. Check which datacentre your data is kept in see steps on how to do this here: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/admin/find-fabric-home-region 

 3. Get the Azure IPs as described below:

  • Download the JSON file listing of IP addresses for all Microsoft services:

https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=56519

  • Open the JSON file in VS code and search for the service (PowerBI.WestEurope) to locate the datacentre: In my case it was West Europe for PowerQueryOnline and PowerBI. search for your region based on step 2 (I added both sets of IPs PowerQueryOnline and PowerBI):

Next thing is to convert these IP addresses into manual ranges that Success Factors can understand.

You can use copilot or chatgpt to do this for you. Both the IPv4 and IPv6 are acceptable but you must convert both to manual ranges.

The ranges must be comma separated into Success Factors:

  • Add all the manually converted ranges to Success Factors API users in   Password & Login Policy Settings: > Set API login exceptions...
  • Select your api user and whitelist the IPs.

I hope this is of some help to you. 

All the best,

Regards,

Daniel

entry.title: SAP Background Jobs: Sales Document
entry.content:
Managing sales reports manually can be time-consuming and prone to errors. To improve efficiency, I implemented SAP Background Jobs to automate the generation of sales document reports in VA05. This eliminated manual work and ensured that reports were always available when needed.

Step-by-Step Process

  1. Create a Variant in VA05

    • Open VA05, enter the required sales organization, and define a variant with relevant selection criteria.
    • Click
    • Input variant name and description
  2. Identify the Program to Automate
  • Navigate to System > Status in VA05 and copy the program name (SD_SALES_DOCUMENT_VIEW).
  • Go to VA05, system, status
  • Copy Program: SD_SALES_DOCUMENT_VIEW

  • 3. Schedule the Background Job in SM36

    • Open SM36, enter General data
    • Then, Click step
    • Input name program and created variant, then save
    • Then click
    • Click immediate and thick periodic job
    • Click period values, choose other periods, input 1 in minutes
    • Then save

4. Simple job selection

  • Go to sm37 and input created job name
  • Click execute
  • Input one line from job overview

  •  

  • Click

     

  • List data will be displayed

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM): A Basic Guide
entry.content:
Efficient returns management is a critical aspect of modern business operations, directly impacting customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and financial performance. Traditional SAP return processes required separate workflows for customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns, often leading to manual interventions, inefficiencies, and high processing costs.

With SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM), SAP has unified these workflows into an automated, structured, and integrated framework. This blog provides a detailed walkthrough of ARM, covering core concepts, process flows, key advantages, and step-by-step configuration.

1. What is SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM)?

SAP Advanced Returns Management (ARM) is a return management solution embedded within SAP S/4HANA, designed to streamline end-to-end return processes across various business scenarios.

1.1 Key Benefits of ARM

  • Unified return processing for customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.
  • Automated document generation, reducing manual effort and errors.
  • Integration with SAP Quality Management (QM), Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), and Finance (FI/CO).
  • Dynamic follow-up actions based on return reasons and inspection results.
  • Enhanced visibility and tracking through SAP Fiori applications such as Manage Customer Returns.

 

2. Traditional SAP Returns Management vs. ARM

Key Differences Between Traditional Returns and ARM

Feature

Traditional SAP Returns

SAP S/4HANA ARM

Process Structure

Separate return processes for customer, supplier, and stock transfers

Unified, integrated return process

Document Flow

Requires manual handling

Automated follow-up document generation

Quality Management

Manual quality inspections

Automated QM integration with inspection lots

Tracking & Monitoring

Complex, manual tracking

Centralized tracking via Manage Customer Returns App

Automation

Manual document handling

Automated refund approvals and logistics execution

ARM reduces process complexity, eliminates redundancies, and improves end-to-end return cycle efficiency.

 

3. SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM) Processes

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management operates through three primary return processes: customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.

3.1 Customer Returns

Customer returns refer to the process initiated when a customer sends back a product for specific reasons. These returns can be classified as either planned or unplanned:

  • Planned Returns: Returns anticipated due to order errors, shipment issues, or product recalls.
  • Unplanned Returns: Returns initiated by the customer without prior notification or justification.

Customer Return Process Steps

  1. Creation of a Return Order (Return Sales Order)
  2. Generation of a Return Delivery Document (Return Delivery)
  3. Goods Receipt Processing (Goods Receipt)
  4. Quality Inspection and Follow-Up Actions (Quality Inspection & Follow-Up)
  5. Determination of Refund Amount and Execution of Customer Credit or Replacement (Refund or Replacement)

SAP Fiori's Manage Customer Returns application enables businesses to efficiently track and manage customer return orders.

 

ARM Configuration for Customer Returns

With the standard configuration complete, you’re ready to configure ARM for customer returns.

Here’s an overview of the most important steps:

➡️Activate ARM for return order types

You need to activate the sales document type to be used in ARM and assign subsequent document types for:

✔️Credit memo request

✔️Replacement order

✔️Delivery document

 

Step 1: Activate ARM for Return Order Types

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for Returns Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The CBAR sales document type is activated.

✔️Assigned subsequent document types.

 

Step 2: Activate ARM for CMR Order Types

📌Objective:

You need to activate the credit memo request sales document type to create a credit memo request during the refund process.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for CMR Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The GA2 sales document type is activated.

 

Step 3: Activate ARM for SDF Order Types

📌Objective:

The sales document type for replacement orders is used to create free-of-cost replacement orders in ARM.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for SDF Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The SD2 sales document type is activated.

 

Step 4: Configure, Activate, and Rename Follow-Up Activities

📌Objective:

You need to define logistics follow-up activities for processing return deliveries, which will drive the logistics flow of the return material.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

✅Action:

✔️Activate and rename follow-up activities used in ARM.

 

Step 5: Activate and Rename the Refund Control

📌Objective:

You need to activate refund control configuration, which will help in defining the controls on refund processing, such as:

✔️Process credit immediately

✔️No refund

✔️Refund through credit memo request processing

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

✅Action:

✔️Activate and rename refund control for an ARM return order.

 

Step 6: Define Return Reasons for Customer Returns

📌Objective:

You need to define return reasons for customer returns configuration, which will help in controlling the credit process (whether to give a refund or not) and other pricing elements.

📌Example:

✔️A restocking fee can be controlled based on the return reason.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Define Return Reasons for Customer Returns

✅Action:

✔️Several return reasons can be defined.

 

Step 7: Configure Default Values for Returns Refund Codes

📌Objective:

You need to configure default values for returns refund codes to control the automatic credit memo creation process for:

✔️Granting credit

✔️Rejecting credit based on inspection results provided by the quality team

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Configure Default Values for Returns Refund Codes

✅Action:

✔️Set up default refund codes to align with inspection results.

 

Step 8: Specify Follow-Up Document Types for Customer Returns

📌Objective:

You need to specify follow-up document types for customer returns to maintain:

✔️Automatic credit memo request processing

✔️Replacement orders

✔️Return stock/transfer orders

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Storage Locations for Follow-Up Activities

✅Assigned Document Types:

✔️NB2 → Supplier returns

✔️UB2 → Intracompany returns to another plant

✔️NB2C → Intercompany returns to another plant

✔️SD2 → SDF replacements

✔️GA2 → Credit memo requests

 

Step 9: Configure Movement Type for Goods Receipt Posting

📌Objective:

You need to specify the movement types for immediate goods receipt posting into various stock types, such as:

✔️Free stock

✔️Blocked stock

✔️Quality inspection stock

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Movement Types for Immediate Goods Receipts

✅Action:

✔️Maintain movement types for schedule line category AR.

 

Step 10: Specify Stock Type for Stock Transfers

📌Objective:

You need to specify the stock types, such as:

✔️Unrestricted stock

✔️Blocked stock

These stock types will be derived from the quality inspection result follow-up activity.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Stock Types for Goods Receipts

✅Action:

✔️Maintain plant-specific stock types.

 

Step 11: Map ERP Delivery Type to EWM Delivery

📌Objective:

To replicate outbound and inbound deliveries in the EWM system, you’ll need to map ERP delivery types to EWM delivery types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Ensure proper mapping of delivery document types for ARM returns.

 

Step 12: Map ERP Item Categories to EWM Item Types

📌Objective:

You also need to map ERP item categories to EWM delivery item types to ensure correct logistics execution.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Configure item category mappings between ERP and EWM for ARM return processes.

 

3.2 Supplier Returns

The process of returning products to the supplier due to quality issues or other reasons.

Supplier Return Process Steps:

  1. Create a Return Purchase Order (Return Purchase Order)
  2. Execute Return Shipment (Return Outbound Delivery & Goods Issue)
  3. Conduct Quality Inspection at the Supplier's End (Supplier Inspection)
  4. Process Supplier Credit or Replacement (Credit/Replacements from Supplier)

 

ARM Configuration for Supplier Returns

This section describes the configuration settings required for the ARM return purchase order process for a plant.

We assume that other Customizing settings (such as organizational structure, pricing, and output control) are already in place. You also need to maintain the configuration of document types across purchasing, shipping, inventory management, and invoice verification.

In addition to these configurations, you also need to perform the following mappings between embedded EWM and SAP S/4HANA (the ERP system).

 

Step: Set Up EWM for Supplier Returns

📌Objective:

To map the ERP delivery type and item category with the EWM document type and item type.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types

✅Action:

✔️Map ERP item categories with EWM item types via:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

 

3.3 Stock Transfer Returns

Stock transfer returns refer to return processes between different plants within the company.

Stock Transfer Return Process Steps:

  1. Create a Return Stock Transfer Order (Return Stock Transfer Order)
  2. Execute Shipment and Goods Issue (Outbound Delivery & Goods Issue)
  3. Goods Receipt and Quality Inspection at the Receiving Plant (Goods Receipt & Quality Inspection)
  4. Perform Relevant Follow-Up Actions (Follow-Up Actions)

 

ARM Configuration for Stock Transfer Returns

With the standard configuration complete, you’re ready to configure ARM for return stock transfer orders. Below is an overview of the most important steps:

 

Step 1: Activate the ARM Return Stock Transfer Order Document Type

📌Objective:

You can activate ARM for return stock transfer order document types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Advances Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for Purchase Order Types

✅Action:

✔️UB2 and NB2C purchase order types are activated for ARM.

 

Step 2: Define the Number Range for Advanced Returns Process IDs

📌Objective:

Define a number range for process IDs to enable process monitoring.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Advances Returns Management → Define Number Range for Advanced Returns Process IDs

✅Action:

✔️The number range interval must be 01.

 

Step 3: Configure the Usage of Delivery Types for Store Returns

📌Objective:

Assign purchasing document category type F (purchase order) for document types UB2 and NB2C, as well as the plant (SPlt).

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Store Return / Return Plant to Plant

✅Action:

✔️Configure delivery document types for store returns.

 

Step 4: Configure the Automatic Creation of Outbound Deliveries

📌Objective:

Set up automatic creation of outbound deliveries for return stock transfer orders.

📌IMG Path & Actions:

✔️Activate automatic replenishment deliveries & CRM billing

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Setup Stock Transport Order → Activate Automatic Delivery Creation and CRM Billing

✔️Activate or deactivate automatic outbound deliveries for stock transfer orders (per document type and shipping point)

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Setup Stock Transport Order → Activate Automatic Delivery Creation for PO Type and Shipping Point

✔️Define profiles for creating and processing deliveries

➡️Logistics Execution → Shipping → Worklists → Maintain Parameters for Creating Deliveries

(SAP provides profile STOA, but custom profiles can be created.)

 

Step 5: Map ERP Delivery Type to EWM Delivery

📌Objective:

To replicate outbound and inbound deliveries in the EWM system, you need to map ERP delivery types to EWM delivery types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Ensure proper mapping of delivery document types for ARM return processes.

 

Step 6: Map ERP Item Categories to EWM Item Types

📌Objective:

To ensure correct logistics execution, you need to map ERP item categories to EWM delivery item types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Configure item category mappings between ERP and EWM for ARM stock transfer returns.

 

4. Integration with Quality Management

SAP S/4HANA’s Advanced Returns Management (ARM) solution is seamlessly integrated into customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.

SAP has developed a straightforward approach to document material inspection in the ARM process, allowing users to enter inspection codes and comments.

Furthermore, ARM can be integrated with the Quality Management (QM) module in SAP S/4HANA for companies requiring detailed quality inspections to support quality assurance and process improvements in manufacturing industries.

How Quality Management is Integrated in ARM:

✔️Use an inspection lot to enter data for material inspection in ARM.

✔️Use a quality notification to:

  • Create a return order
  • Display a return order
  • Display a return order overview

This section will explore how quality management is integrated into returns processing, including its role in inspections and key configuration activities.

 

4.1 Quality Management in Returns Processing

In ARM, material inspection can be performed to accept or reject returned products.

  • Companies can choose to integrate ARM with Quality Management (QM) for automatic inspection lot creation.
  • The quality team inspects the product to determine the next steps, initiating a quality notification process for further analysis.
  • This helps companies diagnose product defects and make precise quality improvements.

 

Material Inspection Process in ARM

✔️A material inspection code can be entered at the time of return order creation.

✔️The inspection process is fast and optimized for retail and wholesale environments.

✔️A material inspection code can also be entered in the warehouse upon goods receipt for a return delivery.

Required Inspection Data in ARM:

To complete a material inspection, the following information must be recorded:

✔️Inspection code

✔️Inspection comments (long text)

✔️Material inspector details

✔️External document attachment

✔️Inspection date

When an inspection occurs at a customer location, the inspection results are typically entered at the time of return order creation.

📌To enter inspection results in the warehouse:

➡️SAP Easy Access → Logistics → Central Functions → Advanced Returns Management → Material Inspection → Enter Material Inspection in Warehouse

➡️Transaction Code: MSR_INSPWH

 

Key Fields in the Inspection Process

✔️Inspection Lot

  • The system updates the inspection lot number when activated.
  • Ad hoc inspections can be created by clicking the Create icon.

✔️Partial Lot

  • A unique number within the corresponding inspection lot.
  • Useful for batch splitting on the delivery note.

✔️Inspection Code

  • Defines the quality of the returned material as determined during inspection.

✔️Comment

  • Allows users to enter comments related to the inspection process.

✔️Inspection Date

  • Specifies the date when inspection was performed or recorded.

✔️Inspector

  • Identifies the person responsible for the inspection.

✔️Inspected Quantity

  • Defines the quantity of the inspected item or split item.

 

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM) provides a powerful, automated, and fully integrated solution for managing customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns efficiently.

In this blog, we covered the key configuration steps and main processes involved in ARM. However, this content serves as a summary, and further details may be required based on specific business needs.

For more in-depth insights or customized solutions, feel free to reach out to us!

📌Your feedback and questions are always welcome!

Thank you for reading! 😊🚀

entry.title: Re: how do I whitelist all the powerBI IP addresses in su...
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I found the answer after a long time of struggling. 

Steps:

1. Make sure your API user is set so the password does not expire as this also results in the same opaque error.

2. Check which datacentre your data is kept in see steps on how to do this here: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/admin/find-fabric-home-region 

 3. Get the Azure IPs as described below:

  • Download the JSON file listing of IP addresses for all Microsoft services:

https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=56519

  • Open the JSON file in VS code and search for the service (PowerBI.WestEurope) to locate the datacentre: In my case it was West Europe for PowerQueryOnline and PowerBI. search for your region based on step 2 (I added both sets of IPs PowerQueryOnline and PowerBI):

Next thing is to convert these IP addresses into manual ranges that Success Factors can understand.

You can use copilot or chatgpt to do this for you. Both the IPv4 and IPv6 are acceptable but you must convert both to manual ranges.

The ranges must be comma separated into Success Factors:

  • Add all the manually converted ranges to Success Factors API users in   Password & Login Policy Settings: > Set API login exceptions...
  • Select your api user and whitelist the IPs.

I hope this is of some help to you. 

All the best,

Regards,

Daniel

entry.title: Transportation Management - What's New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the innovations for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. The What's New Presentation provides an additional overview of all innovations.

The innovations are balanced across the areas core functions, user experience, extensibility and integration, thus providing value additions for the whole portfolio. As always, this release has been a great team effort, and I am more than happy to dive right into it.

CORE FUNCTIONS

Split of Outbound Delivery Orders

​This feature enables you to split outbound delivery orders when using Warehouse Management with Transportation Management. For outbound delivery orders for which you have performed transportation planning, you can now trigger a delivery split as part of the final loading process. When a freight order stop is set to Loading Completed, all handling units that have not yet been loaded are split into a new outbound delivery order.

In my opinion this is a very important improvement of the already existing loading process, because there is now an easy way to split off handling units into a new outbound delivery order if it is realized only during the physical loading that the handling unit could not be loaded any more.

Figure 1: App Load and Unload Freight Orders

Delete Inbound Deliveries for Stock Transport Orders

This enables a business user, such as a dispatcher, to delete inbound deliveries for stock transport orders in Manage Freight Units app.

In the past there was no easy possibility to delete a transportation management relevant inbound delivery when it was created after the goods issue of an outbound delivery. With this feature it is now possible in a straight manner from the Manage Freight Units app in case the inbound delivery and goods issue of the outbound delivery needs to be rolled back.

Figure 2: App Manage Freight Units

Print Form to Subcontract a Freight Order or Freight Booking

​This feature provides a new print form intended to subcontract a freight order or freight booking to a carrier via e-mail.

Once planning activities for a freight order or freight booking are completed, the freight documents need to be subcontracted to a carrier or forwarder. This can be done via EDI Message (SOAP messages TransportationOrderRequest_Out or TransportationOrderBookingRequest_Out) and now also via e-mail.

This feature simplifies the communication and makes it much more efficient to communicate with freight forwarders and carriers that do not offer EDI connectivity as the system can now generate and send e-mails to those with the freight order or freight booking as an attachment.

The E-Mail content as well as the attached print form is fully customizable and offers also the default extensibility features.

Figure 3: App Manage Freight Orders - Output Request Tab

Figure 4: Sample Print Form to Subcontract a Freight Order

Print Form for Loading List

​This feature allows to print a loading list form for a consignment order that is included in a freight order.

​In the outbound transportation process, one or more freight units are planned on a freight order. If the freight units are assigned to different consignees (customers), they are automatically grouped into different consignment orders.

​When the physical execution of the freight order is performed, the consignment orders (the assigned freight units or deliveries) are loaded on the truck. When the truck is checked out, the freight documents (such as the Waybill) are printed.

Figure 5: Sample Print Form for Loading List

Direct Consumption of ERP Organizational Units

A major simplification had been made when setting up Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It is now no longer required that customers create TM specific Organizational Units and keep them synchronized with the ERP Organizational Units, but Transportation Management is now directly using the Company Codes and Purchasing Organizations which are already configured.

The usage of App "Create and Update TM Organizations" is no longer required.

USER EXPERIENCE

Delete Records in SSCUI "Maintain Route Determination"

​Customers can configure the (SD) Route Determination in SSCUI "Maintain Route Determination" (102151).

​However, once records are maintained by the customer, it has not been possible to delete them, even if they are erroneous.

This led to a bad user experience and with this feature it is now possible to delete records which are no longer required or even erroneous.

Figure 6: SSCUI Maintain Route Determination

EXTENSIBILITY

Print Form Extensibility

This feature allows you to adjust and extend the subcontracting and loading list print forms to your needs using custom extensibility. You can extend the Adobe output form in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer using standard SAP fields that are available in the data source of the forms (FDP_TM_TRANSPORTATION_ORDER_SRV) or customer-specific fields.

Figure 7: App Maintain Form Templates

Extensible VDM/CDS Model

​The VDM (virtual data model) for freight documents (freight order, freight booking, freight unit and consignment order) forms the basis for data access within such documents. The VDM is represented by CDS Views which are modelled in a consistent way, exposing business data - stored in database tables - in a format that is based on business semantics and therefore easier to consume.

The Freight Documents VDM Model is now released for system internal use, and can be used for S/4HANA Cloud (key-user and developer) extensibility.

Figure 8: App Custom Fields

INTEGRATION

Enhancement of Freight Order OData Web API

The Freight Order OData Web API was extended to create freight orders for pick-up and delivery based on an ocean or air freight booking item. Freight Orders for Pick-Up and Delivery can now also be changed and canceled via OData Web API.

Figure 9: Pick-Up and Delivery Freight Orders of an Ocean Freight Booking

Figure 10: Sample Code to create a freight order for pick-up

FIND OUT MORE!

An overview about the current and previous innovations for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can be found in the List of "What's New" Presentations.

entry.title: Unable to configure trusted RFC no matter what
entry.content:
dear Experts,

We need working trusted RFC connections from Solution Manager to an S/4 Hana system and vice versa.
The connection from S/4 to the Solution Manager works!

However, no matter what we tried, from the Solution Manager to the S/4 system simply doesn't work. It ALWAYS 
comes back with the following error:

What we tried so far:

- Recreated the trusts in SMT1 for both systems!
- Fixed yellow fields of role SAP_S_RFCACL and regenerated the profile and reassigned the roles. Profiles are in place too!
- Parameter rfc/allowoldticket4tt is set to value both on all systems
- Transaction SSO2 is green
- We tried also using role SAP_S_SM_RFCACL
- Resetting all buffers

PLEASE suggest a solution!
